Core: 나쁜 상태를 덜어 더 낫게 만들다
Contrast: mitigate와 비슷하나 개선 방향이 더 뚜렷
Example: Targeted transfers ameliorated the worst effects of the downturn.

Contrast: galvanize보다 약하고 과정 중심 (촉발이 아니라 용이화)
Example: Lower switching costs may facilitate market entry.

Contrast: facilitate보다 훨씬 강하고 행동 촉발 중심
Example: The announcement galvanized consumers into collective action.
The program cultivates trust between agencies and residents.
Deregulation briefly invigorated the sector.
The new evidence bolsters confidence in the estimate.
Additional case studies buttress the central claim.
The reform expedites approval for low-risk applications.
A shared grievance animates the movement.
Opaque procedures engender resentment among applicants.
The crisis catalyzed a decade of institutional reform.
Financial shocks propagate through interbank exposures.
This case instantiates the general pattern described above.
Remote work accentuates existing regional disparities.
Social media amplifies the reach of misinformation.
The party consolidated control over regional appointments.

Collocation: potentiate an effect / a drug's action
Example: Sleep deprivation potentiates the stress response.
Breach of the terms activates the penalty clause.
The campaign mobilized volunteers across the region.
The sensor actuates the shutoff valve.
Higher fees induce substitution toward informal providers.
Institutional context modulates the strength of this effect.

Structure: X mediates the relationship between A and B
Collocation: mediate the relationship / effect / a dispute
Example: Trust mediates the relationship between contact and cooperation.
The statute regulates access to the registry.
Prior exposure conditions how respondents interpret the question.
Weak oversight predisposes agencies to capture.

Structure: X primes A to do B
Example: The vignette primes respondents to think in distributive terms.
The survey elicits preferences over hypothetical tradeoffs.
Receptors transduce chemical signals into electrical activity.
Banks transmit monetary policy to the real economy.

Collocation: cascade through the system / into failures
Example: Small delays cascade through the supply chain.

Collocation: obviate the need for / the difficulty
Example: A closed-form solution obviates the need for simulation.
Controlling for tenure attenuates the estimated effect.
Top-coding truncates the income distribution.
Emergency measures curtailed freedom of assembly.
High compliance costs inhibit entry by small firms.
Missing data compromises the validity of the comparison.
Local elites instigated the boycott.
The broadcast incited unrest in several districts.
The proposal provoked an immediate backlash.

Core: 이미 나쁜 것을 더 나쁘게 만들다
Example: Austerity exacerbated regional inequality.
Delays aggravated the existing shortage.
Selective enforcement undermines the legitimacy of the rule.

Collocation: vitiate an argument / consent / a contract
Example: A material misstatement vitiates the agreement.
The practice subverts the intent of the statute.
Procedural hurdles thwarted the reform effort.
Repeated scandals eroded public trust.

Collocation: occlude a pathway / view / the mechanism
Example: Aggregation occludes the underlying heterogeneity.

Core: 숨 막히게 억눌러 못 자라게 하다
Example: Excessive licensing stifles innovation.
Applications surged after the deadline was announced.

Contrast: increase와 달리 속도의 변화 (문맥상 부정도 가능)
Example: Cheap credit accelerated household borrowing.

Collocation: advance rapidly / to a new stage
Example: The technology advanced faster than regulation.
Remittances augment household income.
Competition intensified after deregulation.
Certification schemes have proliferated since 2000.
Exporters diversified into new markets.
Evidence has accumulated steadily since the first trial.
The program expanded to twelve additional districts.
The anticipated savings never materialized.
Digitization transformed how these records are used.

Collocation: convert data into / assets into cash
Example: The procedure converts raw scores into percentiles.

Collocation: transition to a new regime / renewable energy
Example: Several economies are transitioning to renewable generation.
The norm evolved gradually through repeated practice.
The practice mutated into a form of rent-seeking.
Firms adapted to the new rules by restructuring contracts.
The merger reconfigured supply relationships across the sector.
The agency reorganized its regional offices.
The firm restructured its debt obligations.
We modify the standard model to allow for heterogeneity.

Collocation: adjust for inflation / the estimate / expectations
Example: All figures are adjusted for inflation.
The ruling reshaped the regulatory landscape.

Collocation: reorient policy toward / the field / priorities
Example: The discipline reoriented itself toward causal inference.

Collocation: morph into a different form / gradually morph
Example: The pilot scheme morphed into a permanent program.
Remote work has normalized asynchronous communication.
Opposition crystallized around a single demand.

Collocation: emerge from / a pattern emerges / newly emerging
Example: A clear pattern emerges from the disaggregated data.
The coalition fragmented after the election.

Collocation: fluctuate sharply / around a mean / seasonally
Example: Prices fluctuated sharply through the quarter.

Structure: X oscillates between A and B
Collocation: oscillate between extremes / around a value
Example: Policy has oscillated between tightening and relaxation.

Contrast: fluctuate와 달리 시간이 아닌 조건별 차이
Example: Effects vary considerably across institutional settings.

Structure: X alternates between A and B
Example: The regime alternates between expansion and retrenchment.

Collocation: swing sharply / back toward / in favor of
Example: Public opinion swung sharply after the disclosure.
Mortality declined steadily over the period.
Enthusiasm for the reform waned within two years.
Inflationary pressure abated in the second half.
The immediate risk of default receded.
Enrollment plummeted after the fee increase.
The economy contracted for two consecutive quarters.
The gender gap in enrollment has shrunk considerably.
Local manufacturing withered under import competition.
Reserves dwindled to a fraction of their former level.
Working conditions deteriorated after the restructuring.

Collocation: dilute the effect / ownership / a standard
Example: Successive amendments diluted the original standard.
The consultation devolved into a formality.
Real wages stagnated for over a decade.
The initial effect dissipates within three months.
A minor dispute escalated into a legal battle.

Collocation: support / growth / the effect tapers off
Example: Growth tapered off toward the end of the period.
The policy disperses risk across a wider pool.

Collocation: align incentives with / practice with policy
Example: The scheme aligns managerial incentives with long-run value.
Agencies failed to coordinate their responses.
This interpretation coheres with the broader literature.
The framing resonates with voters' lived experience.

Collocation: adhere to a standard / protocol / principle
Example: The study adheres to preregistration guidelines.

Collocation: approximate the true value / a normal distribution
Example: The sample approximates the national distribution.
The pattern resembles that observed in earlier crises.
Regional trends mirror the national pattern.

Collocation: echo earlier findings / a concern / language
Example: These results echo findings from the 1990s.
The Korean case parallels the Taiwanese experience.

Collocation: correspond to a category / value / period
Example: Each code corresponds to a distinct funding stream.
Observed shares match the model's predictions closely.

Collocation: simulate the process / outcomes / a market
Example: We simulate outcomes under alternative assumptions.
Three teams failed to replicate the original result.

Collocation: reflect underlying values / a shift / bias

The two measures differ in how they treat part-time work.

Collocation: diverge from the trend / prediction / path
Example: After 1990 the two series diverge sharply.

Collocation: deviate from the norm / protocol / mean
Example: Several sites deviated from the assigned protocol.
This finding contrasts sharply with earlier work.
Local governments opposed the consolidation plan.
The essay counterposes market logic to civic obligation.
These results directly contradict the standard model.
The chapter juxtaposes rural and urban trajectories.

Collocation: situate the case within / in relation to
Example: We situate this case within the comparative literature.
The chapter contextualizes these differences historically.

Collocation: frame the comparison as / the difference as
Example: The report frames the gap as a matter of access, not ability.

Collocation: position the case as / itself relative to
Example: The authors position their approach between the two camps.

Collocation: map concepts onto / the relationship / a typology
Example: We map these practices onto three ideal types.

Collocation: anchor the analysis in / expectations / claims
Example: The argument is anchored in three detailed case studies.

Core: 더 큰 구조 안에 박아 넣다
Collocation: embed the analysis in / practices in context
Example: Economic action is embedded in social relations.

Collocation: locate the difference in / the source in
Example: We locate the source of the divergence in early schooling.
Income correlates strongly with turnout.

Collocation: converge on a consensus / value / explanation
Example: Independent studies converge on a similar estimate.
Judicial review counterbalances executive discretion.

Collocation: equate growth with welfare / size with power
Example: Critics warn against equating growth with welfare.

Collocation: surpass previous levels / rivals / a record
Example: Output surpassed pre-crisis levels in 2023.
The long-run benefits outweigh the transition costs.

Collocation: eclipse earlier work / a rival / the debate
Example: The later study eclipsed the original finding.
Methodological disputes overshadowed the substantive result.

Collocation: fall short of expectations / the target / proof
Example: The evidence falls short of establishing causation.

Collocation: lag behind peers / the average / reform
Example: Rural districts lag behind urban ones in coverage.

Collocation: predominate in the sample / among cases
Example: Informal arrangements predominate in the sector.
We distinguish capacity from willingness.
Firms differentiate their products to soften competition.
The statute demarcates federal and state jurisdiction.

Collocation: bifurcate into two / the market bifurcates
Example: The labor market has bifurcated into high- and low-wage segments.

Structure: X discriminates between A and B
Example: The test discriminates poorly between the two models.
This experiment elucidates the mechanism linking stress and recall.
The paper explicates Rawls's notion of reflective equilibrium.
We clarify the distinction between capacity and willingness.
Comparative cases illuminate the dynamics of policy diffusion.
The framework articulates three criteria for inclusion.

Collocation: unravel the complexity / a puzzle / the causal chain
Example: The study unravels the causal chain behind the collapse.
We unpack the assumptions embedded in this measure.

Collocation: distill the findings into / key insights / principles
Example: The review distills a large literature into four propositions.
We interpret these results as evidence of crowding out.

Collocation: infer causality / intent / a relationship from
Example: One cannot infer causality from these correlations alone.
From these axioms we deduce a testable prediction.

Collocation: construe the provision as / narrowly / broadly
Example: Courts have construed the provision narrowly.
The revision signifies a departure from earlier doctrine.
Researchers deciphered the encoding used in the archive.

Collocation: translate theory into practice / findings into policy
Example: The paper translates these findings into policy recommendations.
We analyze panel data from thirty-two countries.
This chapter examines the relationship between the two.
We inspected the residuals for signs of misspecification.
Follow-up interviews probed the limits of this account.
We investigate the causes of persistent regional disparity.
Reviewers scrutinized the identification strategy.

Collocation: dissect an argument / the logic / a case
Example: The response dissects the logic of the original claim.
We parse the distinction into three separate claims.
The essay deconstructs the public/private binary.

Collocation: trace the origins to / development of / a path
Example: The author traces the doctrine to nineteenth-century practice.
We model adoption as a function of peer exposure.
Archival work uncovered discrepancies in the official record.

This mechanism explains much of the observed variation.

Collocation: account for variance / the difference / anomalies

Respondents rationalized the decision after the fact.
We conceptualize trust as an institutional rather than personal property.
Few studies theorize the link between the two.
The model postulates rational updating by all agents.
We hypothesize that density moderates this effect.

Collocation: suggest that / a link / an interpretation
Example: The evidence suggests that timing matters more than scale.

Collocation: imply a tradeoff / that / a constraint

Collocation: point to a mechanism / an explanation / evidence
Example: Several findings point to a common institutional source.

Core: 어떤 성격의 것으로 규정해 보이게 하다

Collocation: portray the actor as / the period as

Collocation: represent a shift / a class of / interests

Collocation: derive a result / measure / implication from
Example: We derive the estimator from first principles.

Collocation: extrapolate from the sample / to other settings

Collocation: generalize from cases / to a population

The chapter synthesizes findings across three literatures.

Collocation: allude to a controversy / prior work

The committee endorsed the revised guidelines.
Repeated use legitimizes what began as an exception.
The board sanctioned the use of provisional data.
Parliament ratified the treaty in March.
The court upheld the lower ruling.
The authors justify this restriction on statistical grounds.

Collocation: warrant caution / further study / the conclusion
Example: These anomalies warrant further investigation.
We assess the impact of the subsidy on take-up.
The study evaluates three competing explanations.

Collocation: appraise the value / evidence / a property

Collocation: review the literature / evidence / a decision

Surveys gauge public sentiment imperfectly.
We measure inequality using the Gini coefficient.

Collocation: weigh costs against benefits / the evidence

Collocation: verify a claim / identity / the data

These results underscore the value of disaggregation.

Collocation: characterize the period as / the relationship as

The inquiry determined the cause of the failure.
These data challenge the standard interpretation.
The reply dismisses these objections too quickly.
We reject the null at conventional levels.
Several authors dispute these figures.
Rival groups contest the meaning of the term.
The experiment refutes the strong version of the hypothesis.

Contrast: refute와 달리 논파 성공을 함축하지 않음
Example: The authors rebut each of these criticisms in turn.
The government repudiated the earlier agreement.
Subsequent work discredited the original measure.
Measurement error does not invalidate the broader conclusion.
The ruling nullified the disputed provision.

Reviewers discounted the self-reported measures.
The summary mischaracterizes the original argument.
We classify these regimes as hybrid rather than authoritarian.
Responses were categorized by theme.

The handbook systematizes decades of scattered findings.

Collocation: typify a pattern / an era / the approach

Collocation: map cases onto a typology / the terrain

Collocation: delimit the scope / the field / boundaries

Collocation: situate the category within / a framework
Example: We situate this type within a broader typology.

Core: 더 큰 틀 안에 박아 넣다
Collocation: embed categories in / a scheme in practice

Collocation: characterize the type as / the group as

Collocation: configure a system / the parameters / a design

Collocation: anchor the typology in / definitions in theory

Collocation: frame the debate / issue / question as
Contrast: cast보다 중립적이고 논의 구조를 세우는 쪽

Core: 어떤 성격의 것으로 규정해 보이게 하다

Core: 논의를 특정 맥락 안에 자리잡게 하다

Collocation: locate the origins in / the argument in

Collocation: imply a relationship / that / diminishing returns

Collocation: allude to a debate / earlier work

Collocation: elaborate on the mechanism / point / distinction

Structure: X expands on Y / X expands Y
Collocation: expand on / the scope / the framework

Contrast: move to와 유사하나 학술문의 관용 표현

Collocation: pivot to a new approach / different explanation

Collocation: lay out the argument / structure / plan

Collocation: posit an assumption / a prior / a baseline
Contrast: 같은 posit이지만 여기서는 전제 설정 용법
